key_positions: Committed to peaking domestic carbon dioxide emissions before 2030 and achieving full carbon neutrality before 2060.
Investing at scale in domestic renewable energy capacity, including the world's largest installed base of solar and wind generation.
Upholds the principle of common but differentiated responsibilities as the foundational framework of equitable global climate governance.
Supports the delivery of adequate climate finance and technology transfer from historically high-emitting developed nations to developing countries.
Advancing a green Belt and Road Initiative to export clean energy infrastructure and sustainable development to partner nations.
red_lines: Rejects any external interference in domestic energy policy, asserting the sovereign right to determine the pace of national development.
Opposes climate frameworks that ignore the historical cumulative emissions responsibility of developed industrialised nations.
Will not accept externally imposed arbitrary peak emissions deadlines that disregard China's current development stage and energy security needs.
